description_long: This object is a compact, submachine gun-style firearm with a predominantly black metal body and a wooden grip. The receiver and barrel assembly are constructed from dark, matte-finished metal tubing, with visible welds and mechanical joints. The magazine is curved and protrudes downward, made of black metal, and is inserted just in front of the trigger guard. The wooden grip features vertical grooves for improved handling and a natural wood finish, contrasting with the otherwise industrial appearance. The front end includes a simple tubular barrel shroud and a protective metal loop, while the rear has a minimalistic metal stock frame. Iron sights are mounted along the top, with a rear notch and a front post visible.
description: A compact black metal machine gun with a wooden grip, curved magazine, and simple iron sights. The design is utilitarian, with a mix of metal and wood components.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gun, wooden grip, curved magazine, and tubular metal stock.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel opening, protective metal loop, and narrow profile of the gun.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the magazine, wooden grip, and the metal receiver with visible mechanical details.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden grip, the back of the receiver, and the alignment of the iron sights.
attribute_barrel_type: standard cylindrical barrel with tubular shroud
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
